_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave C (a town, not a diorama: EM-147 15-place district town, EM-148 CC0 GLB asset layer, EM-149 district lane network + SIZE 66, EM-150 building GLB swap, EM-124 rigged character GLBs)

resolution.summary: QE gate for the 5-item Wave C batch on build/wave-c-real-city: EM-147 (additive `district: str | None` on PlaceConfig/PlaceState/to_dict, serialized only when set; the 15-place hand-authored district town in config/world.yaml AND the loader's embedded mirror — core/market/civic/residential/farm, plaza + townhall ids preserved), EM-148 (assets/models.ts registry with MODEL_REGISTRY/PLACE_MODELS/CHARACTER_MODELS, null as a first-class stay-procedural value; Model.tsx useToonGLTF + <Clone> + per-instance tint cloning; toonify.ts one-time toon conversion; 14 vendored CC0 GLBs at 3.65 MB of the 15 MB budget, all 14 recorded in ASSET_LICENSES.md), EM-149 (pure deterministic townLayout.ts — district-anchor spine loop + per-district Prim MST connectors, place-avoidance routing, junction patches, zone tints; coordinate-clustering fallback for districtless places; SIZE 40 → 66 with PAN_BOUND retuned; foliageLayout consumes townLayout's lanes as the single corridor source), EM-150 (Structure/Building render registry GLBs inside ModelBoundary — a class error boundary WRAPPING Suspense, so 404/corrupt GLBs pin the EM-122 procedural fallback instead of unmounting the canvas; offline dim + health soot composed onto GLB materials), EM-124 (Villager/Critter rigged GLBs via SkeletonUtils.clone + useAnimations idle/walk crossfade, CharacterModelBoundary + Suspense around every character, capsules stay as fallback). Full gates re-run by QE: backend 367/367, web 376/376 across 35 files, vite build clean. Adversarial probes all green: 15 hostile probes across two throwaway vitest suites (evil kinds, evil district names, legacy/mixed/degenerate towns, orphan-payload audit, double-invocation determinism) + a backend REPL probe (legacy snapshot restore, 15-place round-trip stability, non-string district values). Browser pass on the live stack: 0 console errors, GLB town + lanes + animated GLB villagers render, click-to-focus works on GLB buildings, 60 fps measured. No bugs found; throwaway probes deleted per protocol. Zero blockers, four MINOR observations.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against contracts/wave-c.md by reading diffs and probing, not by trusting agent reports. Rule 1 (free-scale law): grep over the full wave diff (a12a3ee..HEAD) for chat/provider/completions/anthropic/openai/gemini/ollama/prompt tokens in added lines — zero LLM call sites; the only backend changes are additive district plumbing (loader.py, world.py, plus three one-line PlaceState constructor pass-throughs in app.py/loop.py/run.py — technically outside C1's listed file ownership but pure additive plumbing the contract's schema delta requires; recorded, not docked). Rule 4 (additive-only): PlaceState.to_dict() emits district ONLY when set — probed that a legacy snapshot stripped of every district key restores (all None) and re-serializes with district fully off the wire; the 15-place snapshot round-trips byte-stable on the places block. Rule 5 (CC0 recorded): all 14 vendored GLBs have ASSET_LICENSES.md rows declaring CC0 (KayKit, Kenney, Quaternius — all headlessly acquirable sources per contract). Rule 6 (60fps): measured 60 fps via rAF over 3 s on the live scene at the default camera. Rule 7 (fallback invariant): every one of the four useGLTF consumer paths (Structure → ModelBoundary, Building → ModelBoundary, Villager/Critter → CharacterModelBoundary + Suspense) is wrapped in an error boundary WITH the procedural mesh as fallback — exhaustive grep found no unguarded useGLTF call site (Model.tsx's useToonGLTF is only reachable through those boundaries; the module-scope useGLTF.preload calls are cache warmers, not render paths). Rule 8 (one visual language): toonifyScene converts materials once per GLB with caching, unit-tested incl. map/color preservation. Rule 9 (no new runtime deps): no draco/meshopt decoders needed, package.json untouched by the wave. Place schema: plaza and townhall keep their gate-keying ids; 15 places, 5 districts, district spacing laws pinned by test_town_layout_law. SIZE 66 with PAN_BOUND = SIZE*0.75 and shadow/fog retuned. The pinwheel is dead: Ground draws townLayout's spine+connector graph. Bonus: the Wave B MINOR issue (prototype-key kinds crashing the canvas) was actually FIXED this wave — operationalVariant and buildingStyle now own-property guard, and structureModel.ts guards independently on top (belt and braces, probe-verified).

test_coverage.notes: The wave's own suites are unusually strong and QE's hostile probes found nothing they'd missed. Backend +15 (test_wave_c_town.py): district round-trip config→state→to_dict→from_snapshot, yaml/embedded-mirror sync (a real drift guard), town shape gates + legacy ids, the layout law (spacing/centroid distances), full 15-place end-to-end run on the mock path with snapshot resume, procgen-emits-no-district, absent-district-stays-off-the-wire. Web +141 across new files: models.test.ts re-measures vendored GLB bounding boxes from disk and enforces the registry scales against the 4.2 slot pitch (change a model, the test catches the footprint), license coverage, payload budget, clip presence; townLayout.test.ts covers determinism incl. input-order insensitivity, full reachability laws, clearance, fallback clustering, mixed towns, degenerate 0/1/2-place worlds, prototype-key zone tints; ModelBoundary.test.tsx covers the throw path AND the suspend path; structureModel.test.ts (41 tests) covers evil kinds, null registry entries, tint composition; characterAnim tests cover clip selection. QE probes that went BEYOND the suites, all green: 16 evil kinds ('constructor', '__proto__', 'toString', 'hasOwnProperty', 'prototype', '', 10k-char string, emoji, 'house; DROP TABLE') through the FULL operationalVariant → resolveStructureModel → registry chain landing on valid spec-or-null with on-disk urls; evil district names through grouping+tinting+reachability; the LIVE 15-place town with district stripped (fully-reachable zone-N fallback graph), with null/''/undefined districts, mixed half-tagged, a far-orphan hermit place, and coincident duplicate places; reverse-direction orphan-payload audit (every file under public/models is registry-referenced — the suites only checked registry→disk); double-invocation determinism of townLayout + layoutTrees + layoutWildScatter + pathSegments on the live town; backend non-string district values (int/dict/'') through from_snapshot without throw. Honest gaps, none load-bearing: InstancedModel is exported and unit-tested but has no production consumer yet (towns aren't dense enough — dead code until a wave needs it); animation PLAYBACK (mixer crossfade timing) has no render-level test, consistent with the repo's pure-module convention and covered by the browser gate; non-string district values coerce via str() to ugly-but-harmless reprs ("{'evil': True}").

security.notes: No new endpoints, no new request shapes, no new agent-reachable text paths, zero new LLM surface (grep-verified over the full wave diff). The new external input class is 14 vendored CC0 GLB binaries (3.65 MB) — static-served, parsed client-side by three's GLTFLoader, never touched by the backend, license-audited file-by-file. The model-authored-string attack surface (building kinds, district names) got STRONGER this wave: the Wave B prototype-key canvas-crash hole is fixed (own-property guards in worldSpace.ts buildingStyle/operationalVariant), and the new layers guard independently (structureModel.ts hasOwnProperty on both registries, townLayout.ts zoneTint own-property check) — QE probed 16 hostile kinds and hostile district names through every new chain without a throw, and 'constructor' resolves to the generic tavern GLB, not Object.prototype.constructor. from_snapshot coerces district via str() only when non-None — no injection path (the value only feeds Map grouping keys and own-property tint lookups). No secrets, no auth, no SQL, no new file I/O.

regression_risk.notes: Full suites green (backend 367, web 376) and the additive contract held under hostile probing, but this wave rewires a lot of renderer real estate at once. Verified mitigations: pre-Wave-C snapshots restore and re-serialize with district off the wire (fork/replay seam safe — probed); SIZE 40 → 66 flows through one constant with placeToWorld math unchanged, and villager wander / slot rings derive from it (existing worldSpace tests still pin the mapping; browser pass shows buildings landing on their places); the hub-spoke Ground spokes are gone but foliage clearances now consume townLayout's real lanes, so props can't drift onto the new geometry (corridor tests); every GLB path has a verified procedural fallback so a CDN-less / corrupt-asset world degrades to exactly Wave B quality (ModelBoundary unit tests + the build agents' blocked-network browser probes em150-fallback-blocked*.jpeg). Watch items, none blocking: (1) docked point — the wave's visual surface (GLB facings, label heights, lane aesthetics) is pinned by eyeball + constants like GLB_LABEL_Y, so a future kit swap can silently regress visuals with all tests green; (2) preloadHeroModels() runs at CozyWorld module scope — a failed preload on a flaky network surfaces as unhandled-rejection console noise even though render paths are boundary-protected; (3) InstancedModel is unconsumed export surface; (4) the wave's three out-of-ownership backend touches (app.py/loop.py/run.py) were necessary but mean three construction sites must keep passing district — a future PlaceState construction site that forgets the kwarg silently drops districts (the yaml/mirror sync test would not catch it; the end-to-end town test would, for the API path).

frontend.changed_existing: worldSpace.test.ts retuned for SIZE 66 and gained prototype-key hardening assertions; no existing assertion weakened (additions + the contracted SIZE retune only)

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npx vitest run — 376/376 across 35 files; npm run build — clean in 5.81s (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only; the 3.65 MB GLB payload ships via public/, not the bundle)
test_results.qe_probes: 15 hostile vitest probes (throwaway, deleted — no bugs found): evil kinds × full resolution chain, evil district names, legacy/null/empty/mixed district towns on the LIVE 15-place shape, far-orphan + coincident places, reverse orphan-payload audit, townLayout+foliage double-invocation determinism. Backend REPL probes: legacy snapshot restore (district None, off-wire), 15-place round-trip key-set + places-block byte stability, non-string district coercion without throw. Browser (live stack, web 5173 / backend 8000): 0 console errors after load + interactions (1 benign transient WS-reconnect warning), GLB town renders (castle, homes, windmill, stalls, well, fountain), lane network visible (pinwheel gone), GLB villagers with name chips + chat at the plaza, click-to-focus on GLB buildings recenters the camera and reveals labels, 60 fps measured over 3 s via rAF. Evidence: qe-wave-c-town-initial.jpeg, qe-wave-c-castle-focus.jpeg, qe-wave-c-plaza-zoom.jpeg (repo root).

issues.title: InstancedModel is exported, documented, and unit-tested but has zero production consumers
issues.detail: web/src/components/world3d/assets/Model.tsx:149 — <InstancedModel> (the drei <Merged> path mandated by contract rule 6 for repeated models) is never mounted by Structure/Building/Villager/Critter/Scenery. The live town renders each GLB via per-instance <Clone>, which holds 60 fps at current density (measured), so this is dead-but-tested code rather than a defect. When auto-build towns grow dense (Wave B's EM-115 builds 10 progress/round), the repeated-house case the contract anticipated will want this path. Recommend: keep, and wire it when a density wave lands — or record it as deferred surface in REMAINING-WORK.

issues.title: C1 touched three backend files outside its contracted ownership list (app.py, loop.py, run.py)
issues.detail: contracts/wave-c.md gives C1 config/world.yaml + loader.py + world.py (serialization only), but the wave also added one-line `district=p.district` pass-throughs to the three PlaceState construction sites in backend/petridish/api/app.py:101, engine/loop.py:279, run.py:45. The changes are pure additive plumbing the schema delta REQUIRES (without them the API/loop/headless paths would silently drop districts), so this is a contract-process note, not a code defect. Side effect worth tracking: PlaceState now has three duplicated construction sites that must each remember the kwarg — a fourth site that forgets it would silently ship district-less places with every test green except the end-to-end town test on that specific path.

issues.title: preloadHeroModels() at CozyWorld module scope can surface unhandled-rejection console noise on flaky networks
issues.detail: web/src/components/world3d/CozyWorld.tsx:50 calls preloadHeroModels() (and Villager.tsx:79 / Critter.tsx:85-86 add their own module-scope useGLTF.preload). If a preload fetch fails, drei caches the rejection — render-path consumption is safely caught by the boundaries (verified), but the preload promise itself has no handler, so the browser may log an uncaught-rejection error that would trip a future '0 console errors' gate even though the scene renders perfectly on procedural fallbacks. Not reproducible on the live stack (all assets local). Cheap hardening if it ever bites: give the preload loop a .catch(() => {}) shim or accept the noise as diagnostic.

issues.title: Non-string `district` values in snapshots coerce to their str() repr rather than being rejected
issues.detail: backend/petridish/engine/world.py:1939 — from_snapshot does str(d['district']) for any non-None value, so a hand-edited or hostile snapshot with district: {"evil": true} restores as the literal string "{'evil': True}" and district: 123 as "123" (QE probe). Nothing throws anywhere downstream (the frontend grouping/tinting layers are own-property guarded and treat any string as an opaque key — probed), so this is cosmetic: a junk zone name and a junk tint-cycle group. Pythonic-permissive and consistent with how kind/name are handled; recording for completeness, no action needed.

gate_decision.reason: All five Wave C items verified against contracts/wave-c.md by direct code reading plus hostile probing, and every gate is green: backend 367/367 ('367 passed, 1 warning in 29.77s'), web 376/376 across 35 files, npm run build clean. The contract's load-bearing invariants all hold under attack: the fallback invariant is complete (all four useGLTF consumer paths sit inside error boundaries with procedural fallbacks — no unguarded call site exists; ModelBoundary's throw and suspend paths are unit-tested); the additive data contract survives round-trip and legacy probes (pre-Wave-C snapshots restore with district None and stay off the wire; the 15-place town round-trips byte-stable); the lane network is deterministic, fully reachable on districted, legacy, mixed, degenerate, and hostile inputs; 16 evil model-authored kinds and evil district names traverse the full new resolution chain without a throw — and the Wave B prototype-key canvas-crash issue is actually fixed this wave; asset integrity is two-directional (every spec's file exists on disk, every vendored file is registry-referenced, 14/14 licensed CC0, 3.65 MB of the 15 MB budget); the free-scale law holds (zero LLM call sites in the wave diff — renderer + config only); and the live browser pass shows the GLB town, lane network, and animated GLB villagers at 60 fps with 0 console errors and working click-to-focus. QE's probes found zero bugs; the four recorded issues are MINOR observations (unconsumed InstancedModel surface, an ownership-process note, preload-rejection console-noise potential, permissive district coercion). GREEN to proceed to render-sanity + scoped ux-review + ledger/BUILD-doc closeout.
